title: Set slirp4netns MTU to 65520

comments.body: I've played a bit with `--mtu=65520` and I cannot see any problem with podman
should it be the default for slirp4netns?

`slirp4netns` itself still uses 1500 as the default.
But RootlessKit (which is used by Usernetes and my PR for Docker) overrides the default to 65520.

So I suggest using 65520 as the Podman default.
I'll prepare a patch once the slirp4netns available on Fedora is updated and has the `--mtu` option
Fedora 29 (`slirp4netns-0.1-2.dev.git0037042.fc29.x86_64`) seems supporting MTU, although Fedora 28 (`slirp4netns-0.1-1.dev.gitc4e1bc5.fc28.x86_64`) is not yet
@AkihiroSuda @giuseppe What is going on with this?

A few people have reported significant performance hit with the new default MTU of 65520. This caught me today too, I was seeing terrible slowness that went away when I changed the MTU back to 1500. I'm still scratching my head at this one.

https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/6945
https://www.reddit.com/r/linuxadmin/comments/o6e1x6/performance_issues_when_running_nginx_reverse/
@brandon1024 Please do not comment on old issues/PRs. If you have a reproducer create a new issue.
